evidence.rule_summary_from_author: The SPTemplates table has a row for each template used to create the SP. SPTemplates UID, TemplateID, Name, and Version columns SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host. Opal preconfigures concrete Admin SP Base/Admin and Locking SP Base/Locking SPTemplates rows, so successful host Set of these columns is invalid.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.2 General Metadata Group - SPTemplates (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The SPTemplates table is an object table that identifies the component templates used to form the SP. End Informative Content There SHALL be one row in this table for each template used to create the SP, including a row for the Base Template (for all SPs), and one for the Admin Template in the Admin SP’s SPTemplates table. Table 170 SPTemplates Table Description | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|-------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | TemplateID | | Template_object_ref | | 0x02 | Name | | name | | 0x03 | Version | | bytes_4 |
5.3.2.2.1 UID This is the unique identifier of this row of the SPTemplates table. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.
5.3.2.2.2 TemplateID The value of the TemplateID column is the UID assigned to this template in the Admin SP’s Template table. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.

5.3.2.2.3 Name This is the name of the template used as a component in the creation of this SP. This SHALL be the same as the value recorded in Name column of the Admin SP’s Template table for the associated template. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.

5.3.2.2.4 Version The value of the Version column refers to TCG defined versions of templates. For devices compliant with the template versions defined in this specification, the 4-byte value SHALL be 0x00 0x00 0x00 0x02. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.

4.2.1.2 SPTemplates (M) The SPTemplates table is defined in [2], and Table 19 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the SPTemplates table. *ST1 means this version number or any version number that complies with this SSC. Table 19 - Admin SP - SPTemplates Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 03 00 00 00 01", "TemplateID": "00 00 02 04 00 00 00 01", "Name": "Base", "Version": "00 00 00 02 *ST1" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 03 00 00 00 02", "TemplateID": "00 00 02 04 00 00 00 02", "Name": "Admin", "Version": "00 00 00 02 *ST1" } ] ```

4.3.1.2 SPTemplates (M) The SPTemplates table is defined in [2], and Table 35 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the SPTemplates table. Table 34 - Locking SP - SPInfo Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 02 00 00 00 01", "SPID": "00 00 02 05 00 00 00 02", "Name": "Locking", "Size": "", "SizeInUse": "", "SPSessionTimeout": "", "Enabled": "T" } ] ``` *SP1 means that this version number or any number that supports the defined features in this SSC Table 35 - Locking SP - SPTemplates Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 03 00 00 00 01", "TemplateID": "00 00 02 04 00 00 00 01", "Name": "Base", "Version": "00 00 00 02 *SP1" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 03 00 00 00 02", "TemplateID": "00 00 02 04 00 00 00 06", "Name": "Locking", "Version": "00 00 00 02 *SP1" } ] ```

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: The SPTemplates table has a row for each template used to create the SP. SPTemplates UID, TemplateID, Name, and Version columns SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host. Opal preconfigures concrete Admin SP Base/Admin and Locking SP Base/Locking SPTemplates rows, so successful host Set of these columns is invalid.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.2 General Metadata Group - SPTemplates (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The SPTemplates table is an object table that identifies the component templates used to form the SP. End Informative Content There SHALL be one row in this table for each template used to create the SP, including a row for the Base Template (for all SPs), and one for the Admin Template in the Admin SP’s SPTemplates table. Table 170 SPTemplates Table Description | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|-------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | TemplateID | | Template_object_ref | | 0x02 | Name | | name | | 0x03 | Version | | bytes_4 |
5.3.2.2.1 UID This is the unique identifier of this row of the SPTemplates table. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.
5.3.2.2.2 TemplateID The value of the TemplateID column is the UID assigned to this template in the Admin SP’s Template table. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.

5.3.2.2.3 Name This is the name of the template used as a component in the creation of this SP. This SHALL be the same as the value recorded in Name column of the Admin SP’s Template table for the associated template. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.

5.3.2.2.4 Version The value of the Version column refers to TCG defined versions of templates. For devices compliant with the template versions defined in this specification, the 4-byte value SHALL be 0x00 0x00 0x00 0x02. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.

4.2.1.2 SPTemplates (M) The SPTemplates table is defined in [2], and Table 19 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the SPTemplates table. *ST1 means this version number or any version number that complies with this SSC. Table 19 - Admin SP - SPTemplates Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 03 00 00 00 01", "TemplateID": "00 00 02 04 00 00 00 01", "Name": "Base", "Version": "00 00 00 02 *ST1" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 03 00 00 00 02", "TemplateID": "00 00 02 04 00 00 00 02", "Name": "Admin", "Version": "00 00 00 02 *ST1" } ] ```

4.3.1.2 SPTemplates (M) The SPTemplates table is defined in [2], and Table 35 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the SPTemplates table. Table 34 - Locking SP - SPInfo Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 02 00 00 00 01", "SPID": "00 00 02 05 00 00 00 02", "Name": "Locking", "Size": "", "SizeInUse": "", "SPSessionTimeout": "", "Enabled": "T" } ] ``` *SP1 means that this version number or any number that supports the defined features in this SSC Table 35 - Locking SP - SPTemplates Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 03 00 00 00 01", "TemplateID": "00 00 02 04 00 00 00 01", "Name": "Base", "Version": "00 00 00 02 *SP1" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 03 00 00 00 02", "TemplateID": "00 00 02 04 00 00 00 06", "Name": "Locking", "Version": "00 00 00 02 *SP1" } ] ```

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity
